reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND in-page in headless Chrome — real coherent non-degenerate generation; RAG multi-model verified; desktop+mobile layout checked; page-load conformance clean), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (Transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, fresh profile). Standalone: onnx-community/granite-4.0-350m-ONNX-web (model_type granitemoehybrid — hybrid Mamba-2/attention) at dtype q4 produced coherent non-degenerate output — 'Name three primary colors.' -> 'The primary colors are red, blue, and yellow.'; the hybrid Mamba ops run on the WASM EP without abort. In-page (CDP driver, fresh profile -> Download ~576 MB -> auto-init -> Send): the index explained state-space models coherently and correctly (52 tokens, 3.1 tok/s WASM, 0 console errors).
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; tokens stream via TextStreamer. q4f16/fp16 are WebGPU-only; WebGPU is an optional accelerator with an honest fallback to the verified WASM q4 path.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a streamed chat completion conditioned on the messages. The page renders the assistant reply token-by-token and reports tokens/tok-s/latency. Semantic sanity confirmed: correct, on-topic explanations (primary colors; state-space models) and a correct RAG answer.
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT demo: IBM Granite-4.0 is a newer generation than the built Granite-3.0 AND a distinct hybrid Mamba-2/attention architecture (granitemoehybrid) vs a plain transformer.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/granite-4.0-350m-ONNX-web, linked, plus the original ibm-granite/granite-4.0-350m), the task (text-generation), the architecture (granitemoehybrid — hybrid Mamba-2 + attention, ~350M), the distinctness (Granite-4.0 vs 3.0, hybrid vs transformer), quantisation (q4) with the q4f16/fp16-WebGPU-only note, runtime/backend (Transformers.js / WebAssembly), size (~576 MB), and license (Apache-2.0).
rubric.notes: The 'How the API works' snippet shows the real pipeline + TextStreamer usage.

rubric.evidence: Every page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'onnx-community/granite-4.0-350m-ONNX-web', dtype:'q4', sizeMB:576}); a valid current on-device model auto-initialises with no Send-blocking Load click; only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.
rubric.notes: Verified: fresh profile -> Download (~576 MB) -> auto-init ready in the CDP driver.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/generating status is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the transcript is aria-live=polite; the shared loader exposes Retry on failure; a Stop button interrupts generation. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Generation failed: ...').
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: prompt textarea (Ctrl/Cmd+Enter sends), example chips, Send/Stop, sample checkbox, temperature + max-tokens sliders, and the 'show next-token probabilities' button. Ladder adds a tool selector (practical), a run-all-temperatures button (wild), and a notes/question RAG panel (multi-model).
rubric.notes: Ladder: basics (plain chat), practical (rewrite/summarise/grammar/formal/bullets toolbox), wild (temperature lab), multi-model (MiniLM retrieval -> Granite answer, RAG).

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real Transformers.js text-generation pipeline; the streamed answer, the token/latency readouts, and the top-12 next-token probabilities (one real forward pass -> softmax -> top-k) are all from real on-device runs. RAG answer is grounded in a MiniLM-retrieved note (verified: correct standup note retrieved + answered exactly).
rubric.notes: Small model -> facts can be wrong, disclosed on the page; never faked.

rubric.evidence: Every button exposes an accessible name (buttons-have-names pass); status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); the transcript/answer regions are aria-live=polite so streamed text is announced; sliders/checkboxes/selects/textareas are native labelled controls. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.
rubric.notes: The top-k probability bars carry numeric percentages (text), not colour-only.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + logical properties; the composer/controls wrap; the wild temperature columns and RAG panels use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,...)). responsive-check measured 0px horizontal overflow at desktop (1280x800) and narrow mobile (360x740, DPR3), light+dark.
rubric.notes: Mobile download cost (~576 MB q4; +~25 MB MiniLM on the multi-model page) surfaced on the page. Composer/Send buttons meet ~44px min-block-size.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; tokens stream via TextStreamer callbacks (one postMessage per token) so the interface stays responsive during generation; a Stop control interrupts long runs. The top-k forward pass and RAG embedding/ranking also run off-thread.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — generation never blocks the interface. Measured ~3.1 tok/s on WASM for the 350M hybrid model.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, stream LLM tokens off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; tokens stream to the page via TextStreamer callbacks; the top-k and RAG embedding passes also run off-thread; a Stop control interrupts long runs.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js + mm-worker.js off-thread; UI responsive + Stop works during generation; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: schedule/stream incremental token work without janking the main thread
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Schedule tasks by priority so critical work runs first and background work is deferred.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — token DOM updates are small incremental textContent appends (not full re-renders); the heavy compute stays in the worker so the main thread only does lightweight paint work.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: per-token incremental append in the transcript; no long main-thread tasks observed

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive chat/control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — controls/composer flex-wrap; the transcript and columns use intrinsic sizing and auto-fit minmax(min(100%,...)).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile+desktop overflow measured by responsive-check, light+dark

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region announcing streamed status and errors
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; make streamed content live; give every control an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; transcript/answer are aria-live=polite; chips and buttons carry accessible names.

openQuestions: q4 is ~576 MB; a working WASM q4f16 (needs a WebGPU fp16 path) or a smaller export would cut the mobile download — worth watching onnx-community.
The hybrid Mamba-2 state carries across long contexts; a long-document summarisation demo could better showcase the architecture's long-context strength.
At 350M the model is small; a Granite-4.0 1B/micro WASM variant could be offered as a distinct heavier option if it stays browser-feasible.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a long-context summarisation demo (a NEW slug) that leans on Granite-4's hybrid state-space design for long inputs.
Offer a WebGPU q4f16 fast-path variant for capable devices with an honest requiresWebGPU fallback to this WASM demo.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for granite-4-350m (onnx-community/granite-4.0-350m-ONNX-web, text-generation, q4, ~576 MB — IBM Granite-4.0 'micro', model_type granitemoehybrid: a hybrid Mamba-2/attention architecture; DISTINCT from the built Granite-3.0 in BOTH family and architecture). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome: standalone produced coherent non-degenerate output ('...red, blue, and yellow.'); in-page the index explained state-space models coherently and correctly (52 tokens, 3.1 tok/s WASM, 0 console errors), and the multi-model RAG retrieved the correct note via MiniLM and Granite answered from it exactly. The hybrid Mamba ops run on the WASM EP without abort; q4f16/fp16 are WebGPU-only -> q4 shipped + disclosed. Off-main-thread worker + TextStreamer; real top-12 next-token 'see inside'. Ladder: basics (chat), practical (rewrite/summarise toolbox), wild (temperature lab), multi-model (MiniLM -> Granite RAG). responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. Apache-2.0.
